name: cpig-render-01-mesh-field
description: A single CPig mesh→field must RENDER a ray-marched SDF surface in the in-process GLSL conduit. Sequence (RunCommand macros via RhinoApp.RunScript): _SelAll _Delete (doc hygiene) → _-MeshSphere 0,0,0 10 (a closed mesh primitive — no NURBS _Mesh dialog to hang on) → _SelLast _CPigFromMesh (preselected mesh consumed immediately; AddFieldAndDisplay sets Backend=Glsl and pushes — the dense field voxel-bakes → 3MF → device-free node compile → reverted compileAtomsToGlArtifact routes the grid to scene.grid → u_sdfGrid render) → WaitForPenumbraFrame (requireReal, RELATIVE — gates that a real GLSL frame was presented; pumps RhinoApp.Idle so the push/compile finish) → _SelNone (drop the field-box selection highlight before capture). One viewport capture, mode:capture. IMPORTANT: WaitForPenumbraFrame passing only proves a frame was DRAWN, not that a surface was HIT (_realRev bumps unconditionally) — the correctness verdict is reading cpig-mesh-field-render.png (or the dormant VLM). If _-MeshSphere leaves a trailing prompt the macro hangs → Pipe disconnected (then adjust the macro / use a .3dm fixture).

setup.vlmDescription: A 3D ray-marched signed-distance surface (a smooth solid ball derived from a mesh sphere) drawn into the Rhino viewport. It must NOT be an empty grey viewport, NOT a flat 2D overlay, NOT a wireframe or shaded box mesh, NOT just a bounding-box outline.

checkpoints.name: cpig-mesh-field-render
checkpoints.description: One ray-marched SDF ball (the mesh→field) near the world origin — solid, not a wireframe box or empty viewport.
